steps.title: Read the intercept directly
steps.content: The function is already in slope-intercept form: $$y=-9x+9.$$ In $y=mx+b$, the $y$-intercept is $b$.

steps.title: Identify the requested coordinate
steps.content: Here $b=9$, so the graph crosses the $y$-axis at $(0,9)$. The question asks for the $y$-coordinate, not the whole point.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: At the $y$-intercept, $x=0$, and $f(0)=-9(0)+9=9$. The answer is 9.
